title: diasperus subcrenulatus
intro: Diasperus subcrenulatus, also known as the Ceylon plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to provide various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-cancer, and anti-diabetic properties. Diasperus subcrenulatus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to support the immune system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory infections.
